gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) is an empty scaffold bearing version 1.0.0-seed with status 'scaffold — awaiting human authorship'. All six Articles — Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, and Interpretation Rules — are marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. Without substantive BoR content, Arbiter (#190) has no constitutional perimeter to verify at scope queries. The entire governance architecture (Arbiter, Nomos, Senate, Cerberus) is structurally operational but constitutionally inert — every Action Proposal passes the scope gate by default because there is no defined scope to violate.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Relationship to the Bill of Rights ('The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights. Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.') and MSP Purpose ('translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives') — both are unexecutable against an empty BoR.

gaps.description: The MSP Rules section contains zero substantive rules. The document declares '(none)' under Rules and '(none at seed)' under Genome References. The MSP states that 'The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules' — but Senate (#220) has published no rules since the bootstrap seed on 2026-04-10. Nomos (#200) exists as a gating organ but has no rules to evaluate at the MSP + genome compliance gate. Every Action Proposal that reaches Nomos passes MSP evaluation because there is nothing to evaluate against.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Rules ('The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules') and MSP Purpose ('Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication'). The default-permissive state was intended as a temporary bootstrap condition, but remains active ~23 days after seed.

gaps.description: All 25+ monitored organs are oscillating in lockstep between DEGRADED and ALIVE states approximately every 30 seconds, with simultaneous missed_pong events followed by simultaneous pong_recovered events. This is a systemic health-check pathology, not individual organ failures. When a missed_pong cycle fires, every organ simultaneously transitions to DEGRADED and then recovers as a block. This pattern suggests either a Spine-wide ping interval problem (all organs sharing the same check cycle), a network/loopback saturation event that causes a brief universal timeout, or a health-check design flaw where organs are checked serially in a tight loop rather than independently monitored on staggered intervals.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP Purpose ('The MSP is the operational constitution of the DIO') — a DIO whose constituent organs cannot maintain reliable ALIVE state cannot be said to be operating constitutionally. Governance organs (Arbiter #190, Nomos #200, Senate #220, Cerberus) are among those oscillating, meaning constitutional gatekeeping is intermittently unavailable.

gaps.description: The Radiant dream cycle — responsible for memory consolidation, context freshness triage, and pattern recognition across platform events — has its scheduler disabled and health status 'never_run'. Context blocks have 72% embedding coverage (69 of 96), meaning 27 blocks lack semantic embeddings and cannot participate in similarity-based retrieval. Memory blocks show entities with average ages ranging from 25.9 to 36.7 days, indicating no recent consolidation. The MSP contemplates an organism that learns and adapts within constitutional bounds, but without functional dream cycles, Radiant cannot consolidate experience into persistent memory or recognize patterns that would inform governance decisions.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP Purpose ('machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules') — pattern recognition from operational history is a necessary input to evaluating whether an AP violates implicit invariants. A DIO that cannot consolidate memory cannot learn which patterns are dangerous.

gaps.description: SafeVault backup has a documented pattern of failures spanning at least 2026-04-27 through 2026-05-03. Recurring failure modes include: (a) zombie backup processes blocking subsequent runs — two processes from 2026-05-02 remained alive for ~25 hours and blocked the 2026-05-03 run; (b) NAS mount failures ('Operation not permitted' to //10.10.10.50/agentic-safevault-ai-platform) occurring in 4 of the last 7 recorded runs; (c) script bugs (unbound variable ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS on 2026-04-30). The 2026-05-03 run was 'hung_then_killed' — the backup process was terminated after ~4860 seconds without completing. No successful full backup (S3 + NAS) has been recorded since 2026-03-28.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP Purpose ('safety rules that Nomos evaluates') — data integrity is a foundational safety concern. The MSP contemplates an organism capable of maintaining its own operational integrity, and persistent backup failure threatens the DIO's ability to recover from state corruption. While the MSP seed contains no explicit backup rules, the gap between operational reality (failing backups) and constitutional expectation (self-maintaining organism) is a governance concern.

gaps.description: The MSP Genome References section is empty. Genome documents are specified as organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints that extend the MSP. Without genome documents, every organ operates without codified constraints — there is no tiered risk framework governing which organs may take which actions under which conditions. The MSP contemplates that genome documents carry organ-specific rules, but none have been authored or published.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Genome References ('Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.') and MSP Purpose ('MSP + genome compliance gate').

gaps.description: The Hippocampus organ — designated as the Monad leg for conversation memory — shows zero recent conversations. The MSP contemplates a DIO with multiple memory systems (Radiant for world state, Minder for person models, Hippocampus for conversational context), but Hippocampus has no stored conversations. This may indicate that the organ is not receiving Spine events, is not persisting received conversations, or is in a DEGRADED cycle during conversation windows.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP genome reference contemplates organ-specific operating rules. Hippocampus is named in the architectural vision as a core Monad leg. Its empty state means the DIO cannot recall conversations with its human principal or internal agent interactions — a gap in the organism's ability to maintain coherent interaction history.

```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-001-mission-vacuum",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is an empty scaffold with no defined scope, core identity, or prohibited activities. Without a substantive BoR, the MSP cannot produce machine-actionable objectives, invariants, or safety rules. Every action proposal is evaluated in a default-permissive mode with no strategic grounding, and memory/context formation has no directional filter — it accumulates all platform events without knowing what matters for the enterprise.",
      "msp_ref": "MSP §Purpose (translates BoR into objectives); MSP §Rules (currently empty); BoR Article I–VI all empty",
      "bor_ref": "Articles I (Scope of Enterprise), III (Core Identity), and II (Prohibited Activities) are undefined",
      "priority": "critical",
      "rationale": "Criticality: This is the root cause of all downstream strategic alignment gaps; without a BoR, the DIO has no constitutional identity. Urgency: Immediate — until a human principal authors the BoR, every Nomos-scoped action is unmoored and the system cannot self-correct. Impact: All memory/context formation is undirected; long-term coherence, governance, and safe autonomy are impossible."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-002-dream-cycle-health",
      "description": "The Radiant dream cycle is not scheduled autonomously (scheduled: disabled, health: never_run, last_dream: null). Dream phase 2 (memory consolidation) only runs during interactive Claude Code sessions, leaving the memory store to accumulate duplicate, low-value entries (e.g., repeated backup failure notifications, git sync failures) without regular semantic consolidation. Context freshness is maintained by a bash script, but the critical LLM-driven consolidation that connects operational events into strategic patterns is not happening on a reliable cadence.",
      "msp_ref": "MSP §Purpose (machine-actionable objectives require accurate, consolidated memory); implicit in need for operational constitution evaluation",
      "bor_ref": "BoR Article VI (Interpretation Rules — undefined, but any future rule will require accurate institutional memory)",
      "priority": "high",
      "rationale": "Criticality: Without dream consolidation, Radiant’s memory drifts toward stale operational noise, degrading the DIO’s ability to learn from past actions and evaluate whether proposals align with long-term objectives. Urgency: The gap is chronic (weeks of manual-only cycles) and is actively causing memory bloat (recent 6 duplicate backup entries, multiple git sync duplicates). Impact: Nomos cannot rely on Radiant to provide strategic context for authorization decisions; the platform’s self-awareness erodes."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-003-memory-strategic-alignment",
```
